title: Logout endpoint and session expiration handling
description: Implement `auth.logout`, which deletes the current session row and clears the session cookie, plus confirm expired-session behavior redirects the admin panel user back to `/login`.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth.logout` (protectedProcedure mutation) deletes the caller's `sessions` row and responds with a `Set-Cookie` header clearing the cookie (`Max-Age=0`)
After logout, the previously valid session id no longer authenticates any protected route
Admin panel calls `auth.logout` on a visible 'Sair' action and redirects to `/login` afterward
When any tRPC call returns `UNAUTHORIZED` (including due to session expiration), the admin panel client redirects to `/login` instead of showing a raw error

steps.details: Add `logout: protectedProcedure.mutation(...)` to `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/auth.ts` that deletes the `sessions` row matching the current session id and sets a cookie-clearing header (`Max-Age=0`).

steps.description: Add global UNAUTHORIZED handling in admin panel client
steps.details: In the admin panel's tRPC client setup, add a response/error link that, on any `UNAUTHORIZED` error, clears local auth state and navigates to `/login`.
Add a 'Sair' button in the admin panel's layout (e.g. top nav or user menu) calling `auth.logout` and then navigating to `/login`.
Using Playwright: log in, call logout, then attempt to access a protected route directly (e.g. via a stale request) and confirm it is rejected and the app redirects to `/login`.

Manually expire a session row in the test database (`expira_em` in the past), attempt an authenticated action in the admin panel, and confirm the client redirects to `/login` rather than showing a broken state.

technicalNotes: Session expiration cleanup already happens lazily on access per TASK-11 — this task only needs to verify the client-side redirect behavior, not add new server-side expiration logic.
